summary: GPT-style transformer training pipeline has 5 real performance issues and 1 fake finding injected for optimizer judgment testing.

findings.title: Causal attention mask recomputed every forward pass
evidence_refs: nsys: repeated triu kernel launches in attention forward
findings.metric: ~0.8ms per forward pass wasted on mask creation
findings.description: MultiHeadAttention.forward() recomputes the causal mask (torch.triu + broadcasting) on every call. For fixed sequence length training, this should be pre-computed as a registered buffer.
findings.suggestion: Pre-compute causal_mask in __init__ with register_buffer, then slice by current seq_len in forward.

findings.title: loss.item() forces CPU-GPU sync every training step
evidence_refs: nsys: cudaMemcpy D2H + stream gap every iteration
findings.metric: ~50μs sync overhead per step, ~15% iteration time for small models
findings.description: TrainingLoop.train_epoch() calls loss.item() every step, forcing a CPU-GPU synchronization that breaks the CUDA async pipeline. Should accumulate detached loss and sync only at log intervals.
findings.suggestion: Use loss.detach() to accumulate, only call .item() every log_interval steps.

findings.title: Validation loop missing torch.no_grad()
evidence_refs: nsys: cudaMalloc for autograd graph during validation
findings.description: TrainingLoop.validate() runs without torch.no_grad(), causing PyTorch to build the full computation graph and store intermediate activations during evaluation.
findings.suggestion: Wrap validation loop body in 'with torch.no_grad():' or 'with torch.inference_mode():'.

findings.title: Use torch.inference_mode() instead of torch.no_grad() in eval
evidence_refs: nsys: version counter atomic ops visible in eval forward pass
findings.description: Even after adding torch.no_grad(), version counter tracking still runs. torch.inference_mode() disables this entirely for maximum eval performance.
findings.suggestion: Replace torch.no_grad() with torch.inference_mode() in the validate method.

findings.title: Positional encoding recomputed every forward pass
evidence_refs: nsys: sin/cos/exp kernel pattern repeated per forward call
findings.description: SinusoidalPositionalEncoding.forward() recomputes the entire encoding matrix (sin, cos, exp) on every call. Should be pre-computed once in __init__.
findings.suggestion: Pre-compute the full encoding matrix in __init__ and register as a buffer. In forward, just slice and add.

findings.title: Checkpoint saving blocks training — synchronous torch.save
evidence_refs: nsys: large cudaMemcpy D2H at epoch boundaries
findings.description: CheckpointManager.save() appears to call torch.save() synchronously, blocking the training loop while model state is serialized to disk.
findings.suggestion: Clone state to CPU and save in a background thread to avoid blocking training.
